title: Shared Stash & Town Screen
problem: Players pick up items in the dungeon and must immediately decide to keep or drop them — there is no persistent storage that survives across runs. Items found with one class cannot be used by another. Players also have no way to review their character stats or manage equipped gear outside of a run.
analysis: inventoryStore has a bag (10×4 grid, 20 slots) and equipped slots — both are run-scoped. gameStore has no cross-run item persistence. Two gaps: (1) no stash that survives deaths, and (2) no UI for equipment management outside combat. The solution is a sharedStash: Item[] in gameStore (persists like xp/level/tier) and a dedicated TownScreen that doubles as character sheet + stash manager. The LootScreen (dungeon) must NOT have a stash button — stashing is a town activity only.
implementation: 1. gameStore: add sharedStash: Item[], depositToStash(item), withdrawFromStash(uid), equipFromStash(uid), unequipToStash(slot). 2. equipFromStash: moves item from stash to equipped slot; displaced item goes back to stash. 3. unequipToStash: removes from equipped, pushes to stash. 4. src/screens/TownScreen.tsx: two tabs — EQUIPPED (all 10 equip slots with unequip-to-stash button) and STASH(N) (list of stash items with equip-from-stash or bag-withdraw). 5. Header: ← MAP (back to grid/menu), TOWN title, SWITCH CLASS (to ClassSelectScreen). 6. Character card: class dot + name (class color), level badge, stat chips (HP/MP/DMG/DEF/CRIT/SP via buildPlayerStats). 7. App.tsx: screen === 'town' renders TownScreen; excluded from showMainMenu condition. 8. LootScreen: STASH button removed — dungeon loot goes to bag only. 9. combatStore: add townPortalScrolls: number (resets to 0 on run start), gainScroll() action. Scrolls drop from handleVictory: chest=100%, ancient=20%, elite=10%, rare=10%. Max 3 held. 10. GridScreen TOWN button: disabled when townPortalScrolls===0, consumes 1 scroll on press. Shows 📜×N or 'no scroll'. Chest encounter also calls gainScroll(). 11. Main menu TOWN button: navigates to 'town' (free, no scroll cost).

decisions.decision: Stash is accessible only in town (TownScreen), not from dungeon loot screen
decisions.rationale: Dungeon stashing broke the intended flow: pick up items → manage in town. Keeping stash town-only creates a deliberate decision loop: survive the floor → return to town → deposit keepers → start next run.
alternativesConsidered: Stash accessible anywhere — removes the town trip incentive
Stash accessible from inventory screen (in-run) — still breaks the intent

decisions.decision: Visiting town from the dungeon requires a Town Portal Scroll (rare consumable) — no scroll, no town
decisions.rationale: A potion cost was too cheap — players have 4 potions per run and would visit town freely. Scrolls are gated by drop luck: you only get them from chests (always) and elite/rare kills (10-20% chance). This makes each town visit a deliberate, rare event rather than a habit. Inspired directly by Diablo 2's Town Portal scroll mechanic.
alternativesConsidered: Potion cost — too available, doesn't create real scarcity
HP cost (25% max) — always possible, doesn't reward smart play
Town only accessible between floors — too restrictive, blocks emergency use

decisions.decision: TownScreen combines character sheet + stash management in a tabbed UI
decisions.rationale: Players need both views in sequence: see current stats, see what's in stash, decide what to equip. A combined screen reduces navigation friction. Two tabs (EQUIPPED / STASH) keep it scannable.
alternativesConsidered: Separate CharacterScreen and StashScreen — extra navigation step
Stash as a modal overlay on the main menu

decisions.decision: Equipping from stash displaces the current item back to stash (not to bag)
decisions.rationale: Bag space is limited (20 slots). Stash is the intended storage layer. Sending the displaced item to the bag would fill it up during normal stash management. Stash-to-stash swaps keep the bag free for dungeon pickups.
alternativesConsidered: Displaced item goes to bag — can overflow bag unintentionally
Must manually unequip before equipping a new stash item

decisions.decision: Shared stash is a flat Item[] in gameStore (Zustand), not a separate store or SQLite
decisions.rationale: The stash is logically global game state (like graveyard, xp, tier). Zustand keeps all persistent state co-located. SQLite is reserved for future full save-slot implementation. A flat array is sufficient for v1 — no size cap enforced.
alternativesConsidered: inventoryStore: add stash section — confusing with run-scoped bag
Separate stashStore — unnecessary split for co-located persistent state

successCriteria: Items deposited to stash survive a death and a new run start. Equipping from stash moves the item to the correct slot and sends the displaced item back to stash. TownScreen character card shows correct stats for the active class. The TOWN button from the grid and main menu both navigate to TownScreen. LootScreen has no stash option — only TAKE (bag) or leave.
knownLimitations: No stash size cap in v1 — players can accumulate unlimited items
Stash is Zustand in-memory only — it does not persist to disk between app restarts (future: AsyncStorage/SQLite serialization)
No item sorting or filtering in stash view
Charms and runes in stash must be manually withdrawn to bag (BAG button) — they cannot be directly equipped
Town Portal Scrolls reset to 0 on new run start (refillPotions) — stash management before starting a run must happen from the main menu TOWN button (which is free)
recommendations: Add a DEPOSIT button to InventoryScreen so players can deposit bag items to stash mid-run without going through the LootScreen flow. Add stash count badge to the TOWN button in GridScreen.

publicInterface.name: sharedStash
publicInterface.description: Cross-run shared item storage; persists across deaths and floor resets

publicInterface.name: depositToStash
publicInterface.description: Add an item to the shared stash

publicInterface.name: withdrawFromStash
publicInterface.description: Remove an item from the stash by UID (used when moving to bag or equipping)

publicInterface.name: equipFromStash
publicInterface.description: Equip a stash item to its slot; displaced equipped item returns to stash

publicInterface.name: unequipToStash
publicInterface.description: Remove an equipped item and place it in the stash

publicInterface.name: TownScreen
publicInterface.description: Character sheet + stash manager. Two tabs: EQUIPPED and STASH(N).

codeSnippets.label: Equip from stash — displaced item returns to stash
TownScreen tab navigation
Stat display using buildPlayerStats
codeSnippets.snippet:
const stats = buildPlayerStats(floor, level, equipped, classId)
// Renders: HP, MP (from gameStore.maxMana), DMG (range), DEF, CRIT, SP (sorcerer only)

userGuide:
## The Shared Stash

The stash is permanent storage that survives deaths and run resets. Items you deposit stay there until you use them.

**How to access from the dungeon:** You need a **Town Portal Scroll**. The TOWN button shows how many you have (📜×2) or 'no scroll' if empty — it's grayed out and disabled when you have none.

**How to get scrolls:** Chests always drop 1 scroll when opened. Elite enemies have a 10% chance, Rare enemies 10%, Ancient enemies 20%. You can hold up to 3 at a time. Scrolls reset to 0 when you start a new run.

**From the main menu:** Town is always free — manage your stash between runs without spending scrolls.

**Depositing items:** Pick up items in the dungeon (they go to your bag). When you return to town, use the InventoryScreen to manage your bag, then visit Town to deposit keepers to the stash.

**Equipping from stash:**
1. Open Town → STASH tab
2. Tap EQUIP on any equippable item — it moves to your equipped slot
3. If a different item was in that slot, it returns to the stash automatically

**Unequipping to stash:**
1. Open Town → EQUIPPED tab
2. Tap STASH on any equipped item — it moves back to the shared stash

**Note:** Charms, runes, and potions in the stash show a BAG button instead of EQUIP. Tap BAG to move them to your bag.

**What the stash does NOT do:** Stash is not accessible during dungeon loot drops — that would bypass the town trip. Pick up items in the dungeon, then manage them in town.

## Character Sheet

The top card in TownScreen shows your current class, level, and computed stats:
- **HP**: current max HP including class bonus and level scaling
- **MP**: max mana for your class and level
- **DMG**: weapon damage range including all gear bonuses
- **DEF**: total defense
- **CRIT**: crit chance including class bonus and gear
- **SP**: spell power (Sorcerer only — scales with floor depth)

codexEntry.title: Town & Shared Stash
codexEntry.summary: Town is your base between runs. The shared stash stores items permanently across deaths and run resets.
mechanics: Shared stash persists across deaths and new runs — it is never cleared
Items in your bag are run-scoped; items in the stash are permanent
Equipping from stash: item moves to equipped slot, displaced item returns to stash
Unequipping to stash: item moves from equipped slot to stash
Stash is accessible only in town — not from dungeon loot drops
No stash size cap in v1
tips: Stash your best item before a risky fight — it can still be sacrificed on death if it's equipped, but stashed items are safe.
Scrolls are rare — treat each one like a decision. Is this item worth a town visit right now?
Chests are your most reliable source of scrolls. Hunting chests is the fastest way to visit town.
You can hold up to 3 scrolls. Don't waste them on minor gear swaps — save for a truly important item.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial feature definition — shared stash + TownScreen implemented and wired into app router
